scenarios.rationale: The operating system (Windows Server 2012) is assessed as EOL, so patching or upgrading is recommended.

scenarios.scenario_name: Switch to standard Linux Operating System
scenarios.rationale: The current stack (Windows Server 2012, .NET Core, Microsoft IIS 8.0) shows platform coupling that blocks a simple move to standard Linux.
The application is already deployed on AWS, On-premise and uses a portable stack, so ARM-based hosting is a credible optimization option.
The recorded server technology (Microsoft IIS 8.0) is not a target application server replacement candidate in the provided rules.

scenarios.scenario_name: Application Migration to Cloud Infrastructure (Lift & Shift)
scenarios.rationale: The application is already deployed in AWS, On-premise.
The application is already marked as containerized.
The application is custom-built and integration-heavy, so decoupling and refactoring would likely improve modernization outcomes.
The database (SQL Server 2019) is already current according to the provided lifecycle rules.

scenarios.scenario_name: Switch DB Engine to open-source database solution
scenarios.rationale: The application uses a proprietary database (SQL Server 2019), so an open-source alternative could reduce cost and lock-in.
